info.title: Scalar Galaxy
info.description:
The Scalar Galaxy is an example OpenAPI document to test OpenAPI tools and libraries. It's a fictional universe with fictional planets and fictional data. ## Resources * https://github.com/scalar/scalar * https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification * https://scalar.com ## Markdown Support All descriptions *can* contain ~~tons of text~~ **Markdown**. [If GitHub supports the syntax](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/writing-on-github/getting-started-with-writing-and-formatting-on-github/basic-writing-and-formatting-syntax), chances are we're supporting it, too.
Examples **Blockquotes** > I love OpenAPI. <3 **Tables** | Feature | Availability | | ---------------- | ------------ | | Markdown Support | ✓ | **Accordion** ```html
Using Details Tags

HTML Example

``` **Images** Yes, there's support for images, too! ![Empty placeholder image showing the width/height](https://images.placeholders.dev/?width=1280&height=720) **Alerts** > [!tip] > You can use Markdown alerts in your descriptions.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Some endpoints are public, but some require authentication. We provide all the required endpoints to create an account and authorize yourself.

tags.name: Celestial Bodies
tags.description: Celestial bodies are the planets and satellites in the Scalar Galaxy.

items.label: Upload an image to a planet

operations.title: Get a token
operations.description: Yeah, this is the boring security stuff. Just get your super secret token and move on.

schema.description: A celestial body which can be either a planet or a satellite
A celestial body which can be either a planet or a satellite

snippets.label: cURL
snippets.code:
curl -X POST 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/celestial-bodies' \
  -H "Authorization: Basic <base64-credentials>" \
  -H "apiKeyHeader: <apiKeyHeader>"

snippets.label: JavaScript
Python
snippets.code:
import requests

url = 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/celestial-bodies'
headers = {
  "Authorization": "Basic <base64-credentials>",
  "apiKeyHeader": "<apiKeyHeader>"
}

response = requests.post(url, headers=headers)
print(response.json())

snippets.label: cURL
snippets.code:
curl -X GET 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/me' \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "apiKeyHeader: <apiKeyHeader>"

snippets.label: JavaScript
Python
snippets.code:
import requests

url = 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/me'
headers = {
  "Authorization": "Bearer <token>",
  "apiKeyHeader": "<apiKeyHeader>"
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
print(response.json())

operations.title: Get all planets
operations.description: It's easy to say you know them all, but do you really? Retrieve all the planets and check whether you missed one.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set

snippets.label: cURL
snippets.code: curl -X GET 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/planets?limit=%3Climit%3E&offset=%3Coffset%3E'

snippets.label: JavaScript
Python
snippets.code:
import requests

url = 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/planets?limit=%3Climit%3E&offset=%3Coffset%3E'
headers = {}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
print(response.json())

operations.title: Create a planet
operations.description: Time to play god and create a new planet. What do you think? Ah, don't think too much. What could go wrong anyway?

schema.description: A planet in the Scalar Galaxy

examples: A gas giant with a great red spot

x-enum-descriptions.terrestrial: Rocky planets with solid surfaces, like Earth and Mars
x-enum-descriptions.gas_giant: Large planets composed mainly of hydrogen and helium, like Jupiter and Saturn
x-enum-descriptions.ice_giant: Planets composed of water, ammonia, and methane ices, like Uranus and Neptune
x-enum-descriptions.dwarf: Small planetary bodies that don't meet full planet criteria, like Pluto
x-enum-descriptions.super_earth: Rocky planets larger than Earth but smaller than gas giants

habitabilityIndex.description: A score from 0 to 1 indicating potential habitability

additionalProperties.description: Additional physical measurements for the planet

mass.description: Mass in Earth masses (must be greater than 0)

radius.description: Radius in Earth radii (must be greater than 0)

successCallbackUrl.description: URL which gets invoked upon a successful operation

failureCallbackUrl.description: URL which gets invoked upon a failed operation

operations.title: Delete a planet
operations.description: This endpoint was used to delete planets. Unfortunately, that caused a lot of trouble for planets with life. So, this endpoint is now deprecated and should not be used anymore.

parameters.name: planetId
parameters.description: The ID of the planet to get

operations.title: Upload an image to a planet
operations.summary: Upload an image to a planet
operations.description: Got a crazy good photo of a planet? Share it with the world!

parameters.description: The ID of the planet to get

snippets.code:
curl -X POST 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/newPlanet' \
  -H "Authorization: Basic <base64-credentials>" \
  -H "apiKeyHeader: <apiKeyHeader>"

snippets.label: JavaScript
Python
snippets.code:
import requests

url = 'https://galaxy.scalar.com/newPlanet'
headers = {
  "Authorization": "Basic <base64-credentials>",
  "apiKeyHeader": "<apiKeyHeader>"
}

response = requests.post(url, headers=headers)
print(response.json())
